prompt:
A compact mechanical outlet timer plugs into a wall socket, its circular 24-hour dial ringed with small push-in segments and a single on/off override switch on the face. A short cord from a grow light or aquarium light is plugged into the timer’s front outlet, clearly showing that it controls power to the light on a schedule. The dial markings are visible but too small to read, implying 15-minute segments without exposing specific numbers.

The environment is a near-future solarpunk indoor growing corner with soft, even lighting, a hydroponic tub or planter glowing in the background, and clean, cable-managed surroundings. The 512×512 image is photorealistic with gentle stylization, no logos or legible text on the timer, and a composition that centers the timer, its dial, and the connected plug while the rest of the wall, cords, and grow setup remain softly blurred, emphasizing offline automation of lighting.
